goal: Close the 2 adversarial findings from Phase 1 verification: principal-scope the trace read path (IDOR) and harden the SSE [DONE] splitter against in-content false positives. All 4 success criteria and the 16/16 machine contract already PASS — this is surgical remediation only.
why: Phase 1 FAILED verification on 2 adversarial findings only (HIGH IDOR on getTrace, MEDIUM SSE-splitter false-positive). Both have an in-repo precedent or a precise local fix. Closing them unblocks Phase 2.
success_criteria: getTrace requires and applies a principal predicate; cross-principal trace reads return 404 (HIGH IDOR closed).
The [DONE] splitter only fires at a true SSE line boundary; in-content data: [DONE] no longer truncates the stream (MEDIUM false-positive closed).
No file outside the 3 named is modified (no re-planning of passing work).

tasks.title: Principal-scope getTrace (close HIGH IDOR)
acceptance_criteria: A principal requesting a trace UUID owned by the other principal receives 404, indistinguishable from a nonexistent id (matches getPendingAction contract).
A principal requesting their own trace UUID still receives { ok: true, data: AgentTrace }.
getTrace no longer compiles when called with a single argument; every call site must supply principal.
tasks.action: In src/lib/agents/traces.ts change getTrace to (id: string, principal: string) and add .eq("principal", principal) immediately after .eq("id", id) before .maybeSingle(), mirroring src/lib/agents/pendingActions.ts:131-133. Update the docblock to state the scoping. In src/app/api/traces/[id]/route.ts change getTrace(id) at line 25 to getTrace(id, principal); principal already exists and is non-null past the 401 guard at lines 20-22.

tasks.title: Constrain the SSE [DONE] splitter to line boundaries (close MEDIUM false-positive)
acceptance_criteria: A chunk whose bytes contain data: [DONE] inside a JSON string value (not at a line start) is forwarded untouched; the delta is preserved.
A real terminal marker at chunk offset 0, or preceded by a \n byte, is still detected so the trace-id line is emitted before the marker (happy path unchanged).
Single-chunk and cross-chunk split cases still terminate via the if (done) guard at lines 569-579.
tasks.action: Read indexOfBytes (lines 613-624) and the splitter block (lines 585-605) first. After a full-needle match at index i in the outer loop of indexOfBytes, return i only when i === 0 || haystack[i - 1] === 0x0A; otherwise continue scanning. Update the JSDoc / line-587 comment to state the line-boundary constraint. indexOfBytes has exactly one caller (line 587), so the in-loop guard is the lowest-surface change.
